title: Any signed WSF token can create estate-wide revocation actions

affected_locations.label: entrypoint
affected_locations.detail: Authenticated create route accepts any resource kind.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Authorization checks only classification and declared compliance scopes; RevocationIntent gets empty facts and returns allow.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: All RevocationIntent targets are folded into the live kill view without tenant ownership checks.

source: Any caller holding a correctly signed, unexpired WSF TrustToken, including a token with empty roles, routes, and models.
closest_control: Front-door signature/expiry/revocation verification and AdmissionPolicy::evaluate; neither enforces verb, kind, resource-prefix caveat, role, service audience, or ownership of RevocationIntent.spec.target.
sink: Raft desired-state write followed by controllers applying token/subject/tenant/ring revocation estate-wide.
impact: A low-privilege tenant can revoke another tenant, token, subject, or trust ring, causing cross-tenant denial of service and potentially disabling Transit keys/workloads.
plausibility: The standard test fixture mints roles=[] and allowed_routes=[] and still creates resources; policy.rs explicitly admits non-compliance kinds. RevocationIndexer consumes target identifiers verbatim.

candidate_local_validation.method: static source trace plus existing targeted tests
candidate_local_validation.evidence: cargo test -p aog-apiserver --test crud --test receipt --test auth --test policy passed 22 tests; tests/common/mod.rs:56-58 uses an empty-authority token and tests/policy.rs:119 confirms non-compliance kinds are admitted.

candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Possess any valid token under the AOG anchor
candidate_local_attack_path.path: create handler -> AdmissionPolicy empty facts -> store -> RevocationIndexer/TrustRing controller

title: GET returns cross-tenant estate objects without tenant authorization

affected_locations.label: entrypoint
affected_locations.detail: GET handler has no Principal extractor and forwards kind/name directly to the reader.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Reader returns the named object without tenant filtering.

source: Authenticated caller controls kind and object name.
closest_control: Route authentication proves a token but the handler discards the established Principal; no metadata.tenant comparison exists.
impact: Cross-tenant disclosure of workload, placement, provider, policy, topology, token-reference, labels, and sealed-envelope metadata.
plausibility: Direct source trace contains no tenant filter or authorization call; names are low-entropy DNS labels and LIST independently exposes enumeration.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: api_router authenticates the route, but handlers.rs:64-76 has no Extension<Principal> and reader.rs:43-56 filters only kind/name.

candidate_local_attack_path.path: authenticated route -> unscoped StoreReader::get -> JSON response
candidate_local_attack_path.outcome: Read another tenant or global control-plane object

title: LIST enumerates every tenant's estate objects

affected_locations.label: entrypoint
affected_locations.detail: LIST handler has no Principal extractor and lists the full kind prefix.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Range query returns every object of the kind without tenant filtering.

source: Authenticated caller selects any supported resource kind.
closest_control: Authentication middleware only; no tenant or resource authorization in handler/reader.
sink: Full kind-wide list serialized to the caller.
impact: Bulk cross-tenant enumeration and disclosure, and it removes the name-guessing prerequisite for the separate GET/update/delete paths.
plausibility: Direct prefix-range implementation has no principal input or tenant predicate.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: handlers.rs:82-94 calls StoreReader::list; reader.rs:63-76 ranges the entire kind prefix.

candidate_local_attack_path.path: authenticated route -> unscoped prefix range -> JSON list
candidate_local_attack_path.outcome: Bulk cross-tenant desired-state disclosure and object-name enumeration

title: Tenant tokens can overwrite system-owned global resources

affected_locations.label: entrypoint
affected_locations.detail: PUT supplies an authenticated principal and replacement object.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Tenant guard rejects only Some(principal), Some(object) mismatches; object tenant=None passes.

source: Tenant-scoped authenticated caller selects a global tenant=None object name and replacement body.
closest_control: Update guard handles cross-tenant Some/Some but omits the tenant-principal/global-object case; policy is also empty for most global kinds.
sink: CAS-protected overwrite of system-owned desired state.
impact: Modification of global RevocationIntent, TrustRing, Node, ProviderPool, PolicyBundle, or other system state depending on object kind, enabling kill-switch reversal/redirection or estate disruption.
plausibility: The conditional is exact and stamp_update preserves tenant=None, so the bypass is not neutralized later.

candidate_local_validation.method: static source trace with negative-control tests
candidate_local_validation.evidence: Existing CRUD tests prove Some/Some mismatch is denied, while the code has no branch denying principal Some/object None.

candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Identify a system-owned global object and satisfy structural schema
candidate_local_attack_path.path: handler -> incomplete tenant guard -> policy default allow -> Raft CAS write

title: Tenant tokens can delete system-owned global kill records

affected_locations.label: entrypoint
affected_locations.detail: DELETE accepts any authenticated principal and object name.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Delete policy plus tenant guard allows tenant principals when the target has tenant=None.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: Tenant teardown deliberately creates a global tenant=None RevocationIntent that survives the deleted tenant.

source: Tenant-scoped authenticated caller selects a system-owned global object.
closest_control: Delete checks policy and Some/Some tenant mismatch only; RevocationIntent has empty policy facts and global targets have no tenant value.
sink: Hard or soft delete in Raft, including removal of deprovision revocation state.
impact: A deprovisioned or malicious tenant can reverse an estate kill record, restore access after teardown, or delete other global control state.
plausibility: teardown.rs explicitly sets intent.metadata.tenant=None; delete guard therefore skips its only tenant denial.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: admission.rs:281 conditional and teardown.rs:95-99 establish a concrete reachable global target; CRUD tests cover only Some/Some cross-tenant objects.

candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Enumerate the global intent (possible via unscoped LIST)
candidate_local_attack_path.path: LIST -> DELETE -> policy default allow -> incomplete tenant guard -> store delete -> revocation view rebuild
candidate_local_attack_path.outcome: Kill-switch reversal / unauthorized global state deletion

title: Desired-state commits can outlive their audit receipts

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Admission creates a fresh process-local in-memory Ledger and signing key.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Raft commit succeeds before best-effort receipt append; ingest result is discarded.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: Ledger stores entries in a Vec with no persistence or recovery input.

source: Any admitted mutation plus process crash/restart or receipt append/lock failure.
closest_control: Hash chaining and signed export protect an in-memory snapshot but provide no atomicity/durability with the Raft mutation.
sink: Committed desired state without a durable corresponding receipt; restart also resets chain/key.
impact: Audit/evidence gaps and unverifiable history for privileged mutations, undermining tamper-evidence and compliance claims; attackers can time disruption after mutation to erase proof.
plausibility: Construction, ordering, discarded Result, and Vec-only storage are explicit in the checked-out code.

candidate_local_validation.method: static persistence and ordering review plus targeted tests
candidate_local_validation.evidence: receipt tests pass only in one process; no restart of AppState or persisted ledger is exercised. Raft write occurs before receipt(), and Ledger has no storage field.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Caller able to trigger an admitted mutation, optionally combined with process crash/DoS
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Mutation commits; process restarts or receipt path fails
candidate_local_attack_path.path: Raft commit -> non-transactional in-memory append -> crash/restart
candidate_local_attack_path.outcome: Mutation remains while evidence disappears or chain restarts under a new key

title: Caller-supplied sealed placeholders bypass sensitive-field sealing validation

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Public placeholder string is treated as proof that TrustRing/ToolGrant fields are already sealed; annotation presence, provenance, and binding are not verified.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: AAD binds only the field name, not tenant, object UID/name, kind, token lineage, or format context.

source: Authenticated create/update body controls the sensitive field value and metadata.annotations.
closest_control: seal_fields is intended to replace plaintext with a server-generated envelope but skips processing solely on a caller-visible marker.
sink: Unvalidated placeholder/annotation stored as desired state and later available to controllers/readers.
impact: Malformed or replayed sealed state can be injected, causing configuration denial or future cross-object credential/key substitution if a consumer unseals the annotation.
plausibility: Direct bypass exists, but no deployed unseal consumer for these annotations was found in the checked-out tree.

candidate_local_validation.method: static source and repository-wide consumer search
candidate_local_validation.evidence: Only seal.rs and tests reference wsf.io/sealed.*; no current unseal path was found.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Need the deployed annotation consumer or explicit invariant that clients may submit sealed envelopes, plus object-context binding rules.

candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Structural schema accepts the marker and annotation
candidate_local_attack_path.outcome: Desired-state integrity/availability impact; confidentiality impact remains unproven
